assertions.notes: BFF emits json:'Trends' (capital T). The SPA TrendAnalysis class reads source['Trends'] (after the fix from PR #2910). Before the fix the SPA read source['Periods'] which is absent on the wire — the chart was always empty. The seeded test-data.sql has player_stats rows for 8 dates across Standard/QuickDraft formats, enough to produce at least one trend period. A Trends/Periods key mismatch would render win-rate-trend-empty instead of win-rate-trend-chart.

Value correctness for the single-match corpus period. The committed corpus has exactly one match with result=win (QuickDraft_SOS_20260526, player_wins=1). A period containing that match must report WinRate=1.0 (1 win / 1 match). This assertion is required because a zero-filled series (divide-by-zero bug, off-by-one in match counting, or Stats.TotalMatches=0) passes the structural 'series non-empty' check but renders the wrong win-rate percentage to the player — a visible bug every session. The inverse: seeding a WinRate of 0.0 in this test must cause the assertion to fail, proving the assertion bites.

fields.win_rate_assertion: period containing the corpus match must have WinRate >= 0.999 and <= 1.001
fields.note: If the BFF computes WinRate from a different period bucket (e.g. weekly aggregation produces TotalMatches=0 for that week), the seeded response in the spec test must still supply a period with WinRate=1.0 — the manifest records the expected truth, the spec test seeds it explicitly.
